steps.title: Find the causal claim
steps.content: Solís-Ramos and colleagues attribute the difference in top-trophic-level biomass to regulatory status. Supporting that claim requires a mechanism showing how fishing changes the presence of those species.

steps.title: Reject weaker evidence
steps.content:
• Choice A concerns total biomass, not why top-level species differ by protection.
  • Choice B would make the unprotected reef harder to reach, weakening a fishing-pressure explanation.
  • Choice C concerns species identity, not fishing effects.

steps.title: Show the fishing mechanism
steps.content: If commercial and sport fishers disproportionately remove species at the highest trophic level, the unprotected reef would be expected to have fewer of them. The answer is D.
